invariants.name: Cantonal cardinality — one adapter per canton
invariants.evidence: LovableDeliveryAdapter is single canonical adapter. Old per-channel adapters (MG, HR, SH) deprecated but retained as reference. No duplicate canton ownership.

invariants.name: Registry-first — all adapters registered before use
invariants.evidence: adapter-resolver.ts instantiates LovableDeliveryAdapter. resolveAdapter() returns it for all channels. Adapter is registered at module load time.

invariants.name: ID authority — message_run_id minted by canonical id-minter
invariants.evidence: mid-engine.ts calls mintMessageRunId() from @/app/lcs/id-minter before passing to adapter. LovableDeliveryAdapter does not mint IDs.

invariants.name: No sideways calls — adapter does not call other adapters or pipeline components
invariants.evidence: LovableDeliveryAdapter imports only types.ts. No imports from pipeline/, gates/, or other adapters. Single fetch() to LOVABLE_DELIVERY_URL.

invariants.name: Contracts declared — adapter payload contract typed and exported
invariants.evidence: AdapterPayload in types.ts declares all 16 fields including 5 new context fields (frame_id, lifecycle_phase, sovereign_company_id, company_name, agent_number). LovableDeliveryAdapter maps to LovableRequestPayload internally.
